title: Goal-Based Agents
concept: Agents that keep explicit goal states and reason about whether candidate actions bring them closer to completion.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: a simple reflex agent decides what to do by...

options.text: Matching the current input to a fixed rule and acting instantly
Imagining every possible future before moving
Asking a human to confirm each step

exercises.explain: A simple reflex agent uses if-this-then-that rules. It reacts fast to what it senses right now, without deep planning toward a future goal.

cards.title: Where am I headed?
cards.body: A reflex agent just reacts. But what if an agent could hold a destination in mind and check every move against it? That is a goal-based agent.

cards.title: Goal-Based Agent
cards.body: It stores an explicit goal state, meaning a clear picture of 'done.' Before acting, it asks whether each candidate action moves it closer to that goal.

cards.title: Like using a map
cards.body: A reflex driver turns wherever the road curves. A goal-based driver knows the address and picks each turn that shrinks the distance to it.

cards.title: The cleaning robot
cards.body: Goal: 'every room is clean.' The robot considers actions like move, vacuum, or dock. It picks vacuum here because it brings the 'all clean' goal closer.

cards.title: Not just faster reflexes
cards.body: A goal-based agent isn't a reflex agent with more rules. The key upgrade is that it compares actions against a goal, so it can handle new situations no rule predicted.

cards.title: Reflex vs Goal-Based
cards.body: One reacts to now; the other reasons toward a target.

left.text: Sees input, fires the matching rule right away.

right.text: Checks: does this action get me closer to my goal?

options.text: It holds an explicit goal state and judges actions by how close they get it to that goal
It has the largest list of if-then rules
It responds the fastest to inputs
It never needs to sense its environment

exercises.explain: The defining trait is an explicit goal plus reasoning about whether candidate actions bring it closer to that goal, not raw speed or rule count.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A goal-based agent can handle a brand-new situation because it reasons toward its goal, not just matches fixed rules.
exercises.explain: Because it evaluates actions against a goal rather than relying only on pre-written rules, it can figure out sensible moves in situations nobody scripted in advance.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
exercises.sentence: A goal-based agent picks the action that brings it closer to its goal.
exercises.explain: The whole point is progress: it favors actions that move it closer to the goal state, not farther away.

exercises.prompt: Which description was written by an AI to sound like a goal-based agent?

options.text: I hold the goal 'kitchen is tidy,' weigh each possible action, and choose the one that shrinks the gap to that goal.
I just do whatever the last rule told me, no matter the outcome.
I move around randomly and hope things improve.

exercises.explain: The correct one names an explicit goal and describes choosing actions that reduce the distance to it, exactly how a goal-based agent reasons.

exercises.prompt: A delivery robot must reach room 7. A wall blocks its usual path, a situation no rule anticipated. A reflex agent stalls. How does a goal-based agent respond?

options.text: It compares available actions against the goal 'be at room 7' and picks a detour that still reduces the distance
It stops, because without a matching rule it has no move
It changes its goal to an easier room
It repeats the blocked action forever

exercises.explain: A goal-based agent keeps its goal fixed ('reach room 7') and evaluates new candidate actions by whether they bring it closer. That is why it can route around an obstacle no rule predicted, while a reflex agent, lacking a matching rule, gets stuck.
